user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Scheduled Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected initiated for Fabrikam Residences.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-24 14:02 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fabrikam Residences account health

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 14:47 EST
Subject: Follow-up: account health Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: account health Discussion - Fabrikam Residences
Date: 2024-02-15
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), David (Fabrikam Residences)

[00:02:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on account health from our last session.

[00:05:00] David (Fabrikam Residences): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:08:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:11:00] David (Fabrikam Residences): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:15:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:20:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:24:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:29:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:32:00] David (Fabrikam Residences): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:37:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:39:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Fabrikam Residences: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Calendar Pre-Pack Automation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
